rationale: Chan and Mukhopadhyay (2010) is cited in the main theoretical framing of the paper's delayed-consumption literature, not just in a passing list. The citing paper explicitly uses this prior work to support claims about how delay-related factors shape valuation of hedonic consumption and to motivate its own boundary-condition logic around imagery likelihood. The new paper's contribution is positioned as broadening that literature from effects on the delayed item itself to effects on concurrent experiences, indicating a meaningful conceptual dependence.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Chan and Mukhopadhyay (2010) as a key conceptual foundation in the delayed hedonic consumption stream. It builds from that foundation by arguing that delay-related anticipation processes also influence enjoyment of simultaneous target experiences, not only the delayed consumption outcome.
